title: sida pueblensis
intro: Sida pueblensis, also known as the Puebla sida, is a type of flowering plant native to Mexico. It is a member of the Malvaceae family and is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Mexican medicine. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, protecting against oxidative stress, and preventing infections. Sida pueblensis can be prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and it is also used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
